Tanglewood Shield  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: SECONDARY
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 25
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +10 WIS: +14 AGI: +10
Effect: Ensnare (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 4.0) at Level 35
WT: 5.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: RNG DRU
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Charges: Unlimited
AC: 25

Useable at 35
QuoteReply
# Jan 22 2004 at 8:31 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591793450239
8 posts
Had this on my druid since 31, finaly was able to use it at 35...messed up snared myself :P Big time mana saver worth every copper :)
▲ Page top
RE: Useable at 35
QuoteReply
# Apr 16 2004 at 3:10 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592252456344
Scholar
208 posts
I have this shield, and to be honest it's not actually that useful.

It doesn't really save much mana as ensnare only takes 35 and this takes much longer to cast (7 secs I think). In theory you could regain almost 35 mana by medding the extra tick (although in practice it won't be that simple...).

I thought it would be good to save the spell slot, but I find myself usually having ensnare memorised anyway when soloing, because if the first one is resisted I normally want to get the second one off quickly.

Now that my wis it at it's cap, and my disease and poison resists are lagging a bit, I am strongly tempted to swap this for a swamp spider mandible, and hopefully end up with some extra cash.
▲ Page top
RE: Useable at 35
QuoteReply
# May 19 2004 at 12:00 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1652660341379
758 posts
I use the Swamp Spider Mandible atm for the mana and (admittedly) the look. Even though Ensnare is only 35 mana, that can mean a lot when you find yourself in a rough spot with too many mobs. Being able to clicky Ensnare to keep them behind you is a great asset.

If I happen to get this as a drop, I will gladly use it ... but would I pay 13k for it? Not unless I win the EQ lotto and have extra money after upgrading other items. Don't look for the WIS add if you are maxxed, but 25 AC and 15 AGI may save yer butt as well... All IMHO of course, hehehe

BTW: I use the Elder Spiritist's Gauntlets for my rough spots, even though at 64 most mobs resist it

EDIT: Also think about this as a twinkie item ... at level 35 +15 WIS and a clicky snare saving you valuable mana from your little 1k or so pool would be grand!

Edited, Wed May 19 13:00:58 2004
▲ Page top
Nice for Aggro Kiting
QuoteReply
# Jan 06 2004 at 3:44 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592259437143
8 posts
I have read quite a few of the comments here, and one missing use that I can think of is aggro kiting. I for one often kite, as its hard for me to find a group most times, so I am used to kiting. Aggro kiting basically is one person kiting a mob while keeping aggro from the people chasing the mob attacking. With this shield I was able to keep aggro from a chain casting 56 wizard, a hasted rogue backstabbing total between 400 and 900 per shot without crit, and a 58 beastlord plus pet. Best part about this? It was mana free, and let me switch ensare with another dot.

I bought this shield on Vazaelle for a mere 3k, was a very lucky buy as they rarely are seen in the bazaar and usually are listed at about 14-16k. I for one am very happy with it, and will never sell.

Elder Spiritualist Vambraces are arm slot, mana free (right click) Drones of Doom. Druid only. Drop in KC which is located in the middle of DL. Got mine while fighting in Right Court Yard, (RCY) but don't remember what droped them. Got some AC, no other stats though.

is just ok
QuoteReply
# Dec 17 2002 at 1:38 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592253377494
7 posts
This shield is nice - but nowhere near worth 10k

Get an earing of woven bark (1k max on Brell server) and a CC shield (maybe 2k) and you get the same affect for a lot less price - and no big difference in mana. I know, i know - earing only has snare - not ensare - but if you group (as i do a lot) the duration of snare always outlasts the life of the mob we wackin anyway.

Shield is not bad - but don't pay an arm and a leg for it

Penasi
Slackin Drood
Brell Serelis Servier

no you don't

# Aug 28 2002 at 11:05 AM Rating: Decent

When is this shields effect useable?
▲ Page top
Monty tells the group '%T Ensnared'
QuoteReply
# Aug 20 2002 at 7:31 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591751504276
Scholar
73 posts
This is quite a useful shield. As others have pointed out, it can free up a spell slot and has some useful stat's, despite it's "newbie" kite shield look.

I "2-box" (multi-playing), usually a Monk and Druid, and initially had some questions about its' usefuleness and reliability. Well...

Ensnare lasts 8 minutes, compared to Snare's 2 minutes (ala Earring of Woven Bark, etc.), is a 1.75 second cast time, 2.25 recast time, and uses about 35 mana... the savings of mana from a Mana Preservation item is negligable, and the short cast-time renders Spell Haste focus items useless... THIS shield, however, has a 4.0 second cast time, and is ofcourse INSTANT recast!

The resist rates are identical to the Druid-cast variety... around one in 30 or so, despite it's "parsed" resistance of Magic +25 (supposedly, rather high- but *we* know better!).

The Tanglewood Shield's cast-time EQUALS that of the cast/recast of the SPELL, AND it has the identical resist rate of the spell! Not many right-clickable items exist in EQ which have a cast-time which is equal to the cast/recast of a given spell!

Players rely on right-click effects to conserve mana, normally, but all-too-often- a *bad pull* or *add/repop* drives us oom, or close to it... and when you're oom, yer OOM! This shield, like the Clerics' Velious quest Leg Plates, has saved many a LOM and/or OOM group, by ensnaring that last fleeing MOB.

For a grouping Druid, this is (imho) about the best tradable shield in the game. Maybe Solo Druids or purists would scoff at the 4-second cast time and lack of HP and mana, and that the effect is only secondary to the Druid's epic Effect "Nature's Wrath"- and one could even poke fun of its' "plain jane" looks, but I have to say, it was always on my list of "dream gear"- and having paid only 10k for it, it is thankfully now in the "dream come true" category.

Enjoy and Beware.

Edited, Tue Aug 20 08:50:28 2002
▲ Page top
Mana Free Snare
QuoteReply
# Aug 12 2002 at 11:46 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591769795228
Scholar
24 posts
Here's my take on the benefit of mana free snare. Sure it's only 35 mana, but it has other uses.

First, you get a free spell slot. Always nice to be able to expand your repertiore (sp?).

Second, clickable items don't have a recast time. Nor are they affected by recast time. So you can use this item JUST after casting a spell without having to wait for your spell gems to refresh. I've been known, in XP groups to Root with my Warden Symbol, Dot, snare, Dot in quick succession. by timing my spells appropriately.
▲ Page top

▲ Page top
Score
QuoteReply
# Dec 02 2001 at 2:35 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1593361391668
2 posts
I just got one of these bad boys in a trade. 2k, black ice sleeves, black ice legs, and a CC shield. i think i came out a winner. i tried this for the first time and it rocks. So what if ensnare is only 35mana? that's 35mana you save for other more important things. The stats are incredible. i mean 14wis? that freed up some slots so i can get resist gear and stay well above 200wis. The only drawback, and it's a small one, is that it looks just like an average shield. it's a wolf in sheeps clothing.
